title: T-043 — UI: TEC-083 BOM Graph (where-used)
prompt: Build BOM dependency graph view per §7A — visualises FG → intermediate → ingredient hierarchy and inverse where-used. Match `bom_graph_tab` at design/Monopilot Design System/technical/bom-detail.jsx:471-544. Use reactflow or visx graph behind dynamic import. RED tests/ui/tec-083-bom-graph.test.tsx. Run: `pnpm vitest run tests/ui/tec-083-bom-graph.test.tsx`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Graph tab visualising BOM dependencies; supports where-used inverse mode.
pipeline_inputs.details: Render is client-side; SSR returns placeholder. Where-used mode adds inverse edges. Used by allergen impact analysis (§10.2 cascade visualisation).
acceptance_criteria: Given the graph tab renders, when compared to design/Monopilot Design System/technical/bom-detail.jsx:471-544, then nodes/edges + toggle (down/up direction) controls match — structural + visual + interaction parity verified by RTL DOM checks.
Given a BOM with 3 levels, when rendered, then 3 layers appear.
Given where-used toggle clicked, when applied, then inverse edges render.
test_strategy: RED tests for parity + toggle behaviour.
risk_red_lines: Do not load graph library on initial bundle (use dynamic import).
